Net Present Value.definition: The difference between the present value of cash inflows and outflows over a period of time, discounted at a specified rate.
Net Present Value.example: If a project requires ₹100 Cr investment and generates ₹150 Cr in present value terms, the NPV is ₹50 Cr (positive NPV indicates value creation).

Internal Rate of Return.definition: The discount rate that makes the Net Present Value (NPV) of all cash flows equal to zero. It represents the project's expected rate of return.
Internal Rate of Return.example: If a project has an IRR of 18%, it means the investment is expected to grow at 18% per year. Compare this to your cost of capital (typically 10-12%).

IRR.definition: Internal Rate of Return - the discount rate that makes NPV = 0.
IRR.example: An IRR of 18% means the project returns 18% annually. Negative IRR indicates the project destroys value.

NPV.definition: Net Present Value - present value of cash inflows minus outflows.
NPV.example: NPV of ₹50 Cr means the project creates ₹50 Crore of value in today's terms.

Profitability Index.definition: The ratio of the present value of future cash flows to the initial investment. Also called Benefit-Cost Ratio.
Profitability Index.example: A PI of 1.5 means for every ₹1 invested, you get ₹1.50 in present value terms. PI > 1 indicates value creation.

Payback Period.definition: The time required for cumulative cash inflows to equal the initial investment.
Payback Period.example: If you invest ₹100 Cr and get ₹25 Cr/year, the payback period is 4 years. Shorter is better for risk reduction.

Absorption Rate.definition: The rate at which available units are sold in a specific market during a given time period, typically expressed as percentage per year.
Absorption Rate.example: An absorption rate of 30%/year means 30% of total units are being sold annually. Higher rates indicate strong demand.

Sales Velocity.definition: The speed at which units are being sold, typically measured in units per month or percentage per month.
Sales Velocity.example: A sales velocity of 2.5 units/month in a 100-unit project means it will take 40 months to sell out at current pace.

Return on Investment.definition: The ratio of net profit to the cost of investment, expressed as a percentage. Shows how much profit is generated relative to the investment.
Return on Investment.example: ROI of 80% means for every ₹100 invested, you earn ₹80 in profit. This is total return, not annualized.

ROI.definition: Return on Investment - ratio of profit to investment cost.
ROI.example: ROI of 80% means ₹80 profit on ₹100 investment.

Price per Sqft.definition: The cost per square foot of saleable area. A key metric for pricing and market comparison.
Price per Sqft.example: ₹5,000/sqft means a 1,000 sqft apartment costs ₹50 Lakhs. PSF varies by location, amenities, and market conditions.

PSF.definition: Price per Square Foot - the cost per sqft of saleable area.

Units.definition: The total number of residential or commercial units in a project. Fundamental measure of project scale.
Units.example: A project with 150 units of 2BHK apartments. Total supply determines sales timeline and revenue potential.

Area.definition: Land area of the project, typically measured in acres or square meters.
Area.example: A 5-acre project with density of 30 units/acre can have up to 150 total units.

Time.definition: Duration of the project lifecycle, from launch to completion and sellout.
Time.example: A 5-year project duration includes construction time (3 years) and sales period (2 years).

Cash.definition: Cash flow - the amount of money moving in and out of the project. Includes revenue (inflow) and costs (outflow).
Cash.example: Total revenue of ₹150 Crore (cash inflow) vs total cost of ₹100 Crore (cash outflow) = ₹50 Cr net cash.

Unit Saleable Size.definition: The average saleable area per unit, measured in square feet per unit (sqft/unit or L²/U).
Unit Saleable Size.example: Average unit size of 450 sqft/unit means each apartment has 450 sqft of saleable area. Smaller units = more affordable.

Percentage Sold.definition: The proportion of total units that have been sold, expressed as a percentage.
Percentage Sold.example: If 120 out of 150 units are sold, percentage sold = 80%. Higher indicates strong market acceptance.

Percentage Unsold.definition: The proportion of total units that remain unsold, expressed as a percentage.
Percentage Unsold.example: If 30 out of 150 units are unsold, percentage unsold = 20%. Lower is better for project completion.
